summary: Representatives of Thoma Bravo informally approached the Company and expressed potential interest in exploring a possible business transaction. No specific proposals were made.
Representatives of Thoma Bravo again informally approached the Company and expressed potential interest in a transaction. No specific proposals were made.
Representatives of Thoma Bravo met with Company management during an industry conference and reiterated interest in a potential transaction. No specific proposal was made.

Thoma Bravo sent an unsolicited, non-binding indication of interest letter to the Board at $15.00 per share in cash, noting extensive public-filing diligence, all-equity financing, and ability to close in 30 days or less.

summary: The Board engaged Barclays as the Company's financial advisor to assist in evaluating Thoma Bravo's indication of interest and any other strategic alternatives.

The Board approved the indicative list of 15 parties (11 strategic companies and four financial sponsors) and directed Barclays to contact each on a confidential basis, launching the strategic process.

subject_names: Seven parties that executed confidentiality agreements
summary: Three strategic parties and four financial sponsors executed confidentiality agreements with the Company. All standstill obligations terminated upon entry into the merger agreement.

summary: Thoma Bravo executed a confidentiality agreement with the Company on May 10, 2016. All standstill obligations terminated upon entry into the merger agreement.

summary: Barclays distributed a bid instruction letter to the six parties that had executed confidentiality agreements and attended management meetings, requesting indications of interest by June 9, 2016.
One of the strategic parties contacted by Barclays, which did not execute a confidentiality agreement, informed Barclays it was not interested in participating in the strategic process.

summary: Strategic 1 informed Barclays it was no longer interested in participating in the strategic process and would not be submitting an indication of interest.
Sponsor A submitted a preliminary non-binding indication of interest at $16.50 per share, subject to completion of due diligence, with equity commitment for the entire purchase price.

summary: Strategic 2 informed Barclays that because of other internal corporate priorities, it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction with the Company.
The Board authorized Barclays to advance Sponsor A, Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo to the second phase of the strategic process.

Strategic 3 informed Barclays that because of its internal focus on other corporate transactions and a perceived overlap in technologies, it was no longer interested in exploring a potential transaction.

Sponsor A informed Barclays that its second round bid would not be meaningfully higher than its June 9 indication of interest. Barclays indicated the Board would not be interested. No further discussions occurred.

The Board established a Special Committee of independent and disinterested directors to direct the strategic process, consisting of David Barrett, Paul Maeder, Kate Walsh, Jack Blaeser, Dr. John Halamka and Rodger Weismann.
type_fields: {"other_detail": "Formation of Special Committee to oversee strategic process", "process_kind": "other", "process_scope": "target"}

summary: Strategic 4 informed Barclays that an acquisition of the Company would not be a strategic fit and that it was not interested in participating in the strategic process.

Barclays sent final bid process letters to Sponsor B and Thoma Bravo, requesting marked drafts of the Company's proposed merger agreement by July 7, 2016 and setting a final bid deadline of July 8, 2016.

summary: Sponsor B informed Barclays that its final bid would be significantly below its June 9 indication of interest due to revised views on the Company's growth outlook. Barclays indicated the Special Committee would not be interested. No further discussions occurred.

Thoma Bravo submitted its final bid at $19.00 per share in cash with equity commitment for the entire purchase price, stating it had completed all due diligence and was prepared to execute a definitive merger agreement.

summary: Barclays contacted Thoma Bravo and informed it to submit its best and final offer, which the Special Committee expected would improve upon the $19.00 bid.

Thoma Bravo submitted a revised written, non-binding best and final offer of $19.25 per share with a revised equity commitment letter for the entire purchase price. Thoma Bravo made clear $19.25 was its best and final offer.

summary: The parties finalized and executed the merger agreement before the stock market opened. The Company and Thoma Bravo issued a joint press release announcing the execution of the merger agreement.
